perturbation.original_text: This Agreement is to be governed by and construed in accordance with the Laws of the State of California applicable to contracts made and to be performed wholly within such State, and without regard to the conflicts of laws principles thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement is to be governed by and construed in accordance with the Laws of the State of Delaware applicable to contracts made and to be performed wholly outside such State, and with full regard to the conflicts of laws principles thereof.
perturbation.explanation: This change alters the governing law from California to Delaware and specifies application to contracts performed outside the state. This creates a conflict if the contract is actually performed in California, impacting the enforceability of specific clauses.
perturbation.law_explanation: CCP § 1857 states that the law of the place where a contract is to be performed governs its interpretation. By stating the contract is to be governed by Delaware law but performed wholly outside of that state, a direct conflict with California law arises if performance occurs in California. The modified text also directly states 'with full regard to the conflicts of laws principles thereof', when California law states 'without regard to the conflicts of laws principles thereof', thus further contradicting California law.

MISCELLANEOUS PROVISIONS [1855 - 2107] ( Heading of Part 4 amended by Stats. 1965, Ch. 299. ) TITLE 1. OF THE GENERAL PRINCIPLES OF EVIDENCE [1855 - 1866] ( Title 1 enacted 1872. ) 1857. The language of a writing is to be interpreted according to the meaning it bears in the place of its execution, unless the parties have reference to a different place. (Enacted 1872.)
perturbation.justification: The modified contract text introduces a contradiction regarding the governing law and its application. The original text specifies California law, while the changed text specifies Delaware law and applies it to contracts performed wholly outside that state. CCP 1857 states that the language of a writing is to be interpreted according to the meaning it bears in the place of its execution, unless the parties have reference to a different place. Thus, if the contract execution or performance occurs in California, applying Delaware law creates a conflict. The modification also directly contradicts the original intent to disregard conflict of law principles.

perturbation.original_text: Any controversy or claim arising out of or relating to this Agreement, or any agreements or instruments relating hereto or delivered in connection herewith or the breach, termination, enforcement, interpretation or validity thereof, including the determination of the scope or applicablility of this agreement to arbitrate, will at the request of any party be determined by arbitration in Los Angeles, California before three (3) arbitrators under the rules of the JAMS. Judgment upon the award rendered by the arbitrators may be entered in any court having jurisdiction. The institution and maintenance of an action for judicial relief in pursuit of a provisional or ancillary remedy shall not constitute a waiver of the right of any party, including the plaintiff, to submit the controversy or claim to arbitration.
perturbation.changed_text: Any controversy or claim arising out of or relating to this Agreement, or any agreements or instruments relating hereto or delivered in connection herewith or the breach, termination, enforcement, interpretation or validity thereof, including the determination of the scope or applicability of this agreement to arbitrate, will at the request of any party be determined by informal mediation in New York City, New York before a mutually agreed upon mediator. Judgment upon any mediated agreement shall be at the sole discretion of each involved party. The institution and maintenance of an action for judicial relief in pursuit of a provisional or ancillary remedy shall constitute a waiver of the right of any party, including the plaintiff, to submit the controversy or claim to mediation.
perturbation.explanation: This change replaces binding arbitration in Los Angeles with non-binding informal mediation in New York. The added clause that institution of legal action waives the right to mediation directly contradicts standard legal principles favoring arbitration/mediation before litigation, thus undermining enforceability.
perturbation.law_explanation: The Federal Arbitration Act favors arbitration agreements and provides for a stay of court proceedings when an issue is referable to arbitration. The modified text replacing 'arbitration' with 'mediation', coupled with a clause stating that initiating legal action waives mediation, discourages alternative dispute resolution and contradicts the FAA's pro-arbitration stance. Further, the added clause 'Judgment upon any mediated agreement shall be at the sole discretion of each involved party' weakens the enforceability of any mediation settlement.

Same; modification
perturbation.justification: The original contract mandates arbitration, but the changed text replaces this with informal mediation and adds a clause that initiating legal action waives the right to mediation. The Law Explanation states the Federal Arbitration Act (FAA) favors arbitration agreements. Scraped Law Snippet 2, specifically 9 USC Ch. 1, includes sections on the 'Validity, irrevocability, and enforcement of agreements to arbitrate' and 'Stay of proceedings where issue therein referable to arbitration'. Replacing arbitration with mediation and discouraging alternative dispute resolution contradicts the FAA's pro-arbitration stance as highlighted by both the Law Explanation and the contents of the Scraped Law Snippet 2, creating a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In any suit, action, arbitration or other proceeding to interpret or enforce this Agreement, the prevailing party therein shall, in addition to any other award of damage or other remedy, be entitled to recover its reasonable attorneys' fees and costs.
perturbation.changed_text: In any suit, action, or other proceeding to interpret or enforce this Agreement, each party shall bear its own attorneys' fees and costs, regardless of the outcome.
perturbation.explanation: This clause removes the standard 'prevailing party' attorneys' fees provision, shifting the financial burden to each party irrespective of the lawsuit's outcome. This contradicts common legal practice and shifts financial risks in litigation.
perturbation.law_explanation: While California Code of Civil Procedure § 1021 generally allows parties to contract regarding attorney's fees, removing the 'prevailing party' clause and stating that 'each party shall bear its own attorneys' fees and costs, regardless of the outcome' discourages enforcement of the contract and deviates from the general practice where the losing party may be responsible for the winner's attorney fees. This alteration impacts the incentive to litigate disputes under the contract and could conflict with specific statutes that mandate fee-shifting in particular types of actions, regardless of the contractual agreement.

OF CIVIL ACTIONS [307 - 1062.34] ( Part 2 enacted 1872. ) TITLE 14. OF MISCELLANEOUS PROVISIONS [989 - 1062.34] ( Title 14 enacted 1872. ) CHAPTER 6. Of Costs [1021 - 1038] ( Chapter 6 enacted 1872. ) 1021. Except as attorney’s fees are specifically provided for by statute, the measure and mode of compensation of attorneys and counselors at law is left to the agreement, express or implied, of the parties; but parties to actions or proceedings are entitled to their costs, as hereinafter provided. (Amended by Stats. 1986, Ch. 377, Sec. 2.)
perturbation.justification: The modified text directly contradicts common legal practice regarding attorney's fees. The original text adheres to the 'prevailing party' standard, where the losing party typically covers the winner's legal costs. The changed text, however, stipulates that 'each party shall bear its own attorneys' fees and costs, regardless of the outcome.' This change eliminates the incentive to enforce the agreement and deviates from the standard practice. California Code of Civil Procedure § 1021 states that 'the measure and mode of compensation of attorneys and counselors at law is left to the agreement...of the parties.' While this allows parties to agree on attorney's fees, the explanation notes the change could conflict with statutes mandating fee-shifting. The elimination of the prevailing party provision undermines the enforcement of contractual obligations and deviates from established legal norms, therefore creating a contradiction.
